comment_content:
It consists of a paper format of quiz and assignments ,which require submission of coded excel file, and final exam .For assignment, you just need to follow the steps written on the password protected website specialized for this course then you will get exactly expected results.
The content was very exciting and every homework for excel coding was very practical and fun to do. i still remember an assignment using excel coding to play the piano. As this was my first coding experience, it gave me the feeling that coding is actually amazing.
comment_teaching: The mode of delivery was great the teacher's voice is calm and she focuses on understanding the mechanisms behind the code slowly so that listeners feel easy to get the concept. Teaching assistants were helpful if asking questions.
comment_workload: Workload is not high as assignments only need to follow the guidelines anf submit the results then get good marks.But make sure you have loads of practice of hard codes before final exam
comment_grading: It was my first class that i got A- so if you do it with passion then high results could be obtained reletively easily. Final exam was hard, especially the last question which may require deep understaning of every concept and repeated practice.

comment_content:
Basic uses of Excel and coding with VBA.
The excel part is very useful. Using VBA can also facilitate data handling in excel. However, for coding, it is not useful as other popular programming languages like Python and C++. For SENG students, I recommend to enroll in COMP1021 instead.
comment_teaching: Very good.
comment_workload:
Very light.
Every week a lab, but not all lab are need to be graded (only 4 out of 8).
comment_grading:
Excellent (for this semester).
Due to social unrest, the final exam is cancelled. Only the 4 labs and midterm exam is used to calculate the overall score. Since the midterm exam is very easy, it is easy to get 95+ in overall score (which must be in A range).

comment_content: The content is pretty easy to understand. First part is the basic of the usage of excel, then second part is dive into the topic of VBA.
comment_teaching: The teaching is lively and David explain the topics is easy to understand. If you have any question in the class, you can find him after class, he will stay there for some minute to answer student questions.
comment_workload: The labs are not too bad, the TA in the labs are very helpful, some of the labs even guide you through the tasks, almost copy and paste.
comment_grading: Exams are open book and notes, the questions are straight forward, not too hard.

comment_content: The content is easy to get. Basic Excel formulas and VBA programs. Very easy for people who have taken ICT as their electives in secondary.

comment_grading: The mean is quite high (>90) for lab work, which means you must be very careful in answering the question. Work harder for the midterm and exam to score an A.

comment_content: This course is designed to equip students with the fundamental concepts of programming using the VBA programming language, within the context of the Microsoft Excel program. Students will first learn how to use Excel to analyze and present data, and will then learn how to use VBA code to build powerful programs.
comment_teaching: Good teaching, very humourous, always asking questions in class which keeps you brainstorming. The questions are good for clearing your concepts. If you can understand every question in class, there is no problem for you in the final exam.
comment_workload: light workload, just some labs and the TAs will help you out.
comment_grading: Good grading.

comment_content:
This class especially, VBA is very interesting at the moment  as I have not finished this course haha.
Students who likes to solve logical problems will like the course for sure.

As I am not a student with CS-related background, this is my only COMP course in my u life. I would say it is a relatively simple course, easy to understand, train more on your thinking skills rather than memorising things.
comment_teaching:
I am in Gibson class. He is a funny guy, but I think he talked a little bit fast, I could not catch up with his pace (it may be my problem)

Thanks to the online lectures, I can join other lecturers' section. From then, I started attending Cecia's class. I really enjoy her class, she is very patient and so nice. She may include some warm up exercise to help us review the previous lecture content. In general, I really like her teaching. I think her class is very suitable for students who may need more time to digest the concepts.

Also, Peter, our TA, is very nice too. He is delivering the lab content very clearly. and I rmb I emailed him for enquiries, then he replied me at 6am the other day. very responsive and helpful!
comment_workload:
pretty light.
4 labs, midterm and final exam
labs: just pay attention to the TA, then you will get everything done within 1 hour for each lab.
comment_grading:
I have not finished the course, I will update another review at the end of this semester.

for exam, usually exam is more difficult than the practice tests and review questions.

comment_content: First few lessons are about basic use of excel worksheet. Content of vba starts at around week 2 or week 3. It is a little bit hard for vba part. Make sure you ask when you do not understand as some concepts builds up from the previous concepts.
comment_teaching: Cecia is a nice instructor. Feel free to ask her questions after lesson or through email.
comment_workload: Only 5 grading labs + 1 midterm + 1 final
comment_grading: The grade is quite good. There are a few grading labs and please do all of it as it is a good way to obtain marks. For the first 4 labs, you can just follow the lab video and copy the steps. The last lab requires you to finish it on your own. Midterm is easier when compared to the final.

comment_content:
The first few lessons were focusing on how to use excel and basic cell functions such as average, vlookup and hlookup. Then the next few classes introduce some basic VBA and how to type VBA codes. After midterm, the content gets a bit harder but still manageable.
Good course for people who are interested in coding or problem-solving.
comment_teaching: Cecia is a very nice instructor. She tries to answer all students' questions and doesn't mind repeating herself. The TA (Peter) is also very nice. He does all the lab recordings and usually explains the process very well, so the first few labs were easy. But for the last few labs, he will leave some blanks for you to fill in and finish the codes yourself.
comment_workload: The workload is light, only remember to hand in the labs and should be fine. No attendance required.
comment_grading:
Grading is fair.
There are a total of 5 graded labs. I highly recommend doing all the labs (including the non-graded ones) to have more practice writing the VBA codes. Most people get full marks on the labs, so make sure you don't lose marks or make stupid mistakes. You can always check if your code is correct by running the VBA code.
Midterm exam was quite straightforward.
Final exam was harder, so remember to revise all the lecture notes and have more practice.
I got full marks for the labs, above mean for midterm and finals. Received A in the end.

comment_content:
This course will teach you basic stuff about Excel like cell formulas. You will also learn basic Excel VBA coding.
The contents are quite practical, and I am sure that you will find it useful in your future job. Highly recommend taking this course!!!
comment_teaching: Gibson is such a nice and funny person. He could teach things clearly and in a fun way at the same time. Those TAs are also really nice. They always answer questions in detail.
comment_workload:
Actually, there was no assignment but labs. Some labs were required to be finished while some didn't. However, I do recommend finishing all the labs as it will be useful for your midterm and final.
The time spent on each lab was about 2 hours on average. Most of the answers of the labs were provided in the tutorial video. Therefore, the means of the required labs were nearly 100 marks.
comment_grading:
The grading is quite nice. I got full marks in all required labs. As for midterms and finals, my marks were above mean about 1 SD on average. I got an A- eventually.
(Don't be afraid when you see your marks! The mean is relatively low compared to the busi courses I have taken.)

comment_content: vba basics, not practical in workplace, but you can learn basic vba.
comment_teaching: very good, they are all very nice and good tutor and professors, which you can have a very nice time in learning.
comment_workload: 6 Labs, need to go to labs to do, but they are easy. Mid term and Final exam.
comment_grading: A, very easy. the a range is large, many students can get a A- or above in this course. labs almost 100, midterm and final only 67 and 78, but still get a A in grade, which is better than java and python.

comment_content: VBA programming for beginners. Even you are not familiar with programming at all, you will not think that this course is difficult. Also, the content is very valuable and useful for business student. You will be learning how to use vba programming language to create macros, draw shapes in excel, etc.
comment_teaching: The teaching is fun, you will never feel bored in his lecture. He always tried his best to explain the content so that even the beginners can understand. You will definitely enjoy the jokes he tells in lectures. Also, Gibson and the teaching assistants are always willing to respond to your questions, so that you can truly understand the content. Once, I encountered some problems when I was finishing my lab assignment, I emailed the teaching assistant and he gave me a clear explanation within a day.
comment_workload: The workload is not heavy at all. You have to submit some lab assignments in the semester. However, the assignments are not difficult at all.
comment_grading: The grading is fair. You just have to study hard and you will get a nice grade. If you work hard, the content in midterm and final will not be challenging at all.

comment_content:
Basic knowledge of VBA in excel.

comment_workload:
Workload is minimum. Have some labs but its ok because you can follow the tutorial video.

Exam and Mid term are alright if you understand all the lab.
comment_grading: I got B+

comment_workload: There's not much work to be done for this course. Besides midterm and final exams there are only assignments, and you do not need to spend a lot of time revising for the exams.
comment_grading: The grades are kinda lenient. Did mediocre but scored a B+ in the end. As long as you finish your assignments on time and don't too bad on the midterm and final exam you should be good to go.

comment_content:
Most of the contents before mid-term are about the basic excel formulas. After that, all is about VBA.

I think it is easy for students to handle the first part(excel formulas) cause it is just easy to understand.
However, it is quite challenging when learning the VBA if you are not familiar with computer stuff. So do the lab exercises and examples again to make sure you really understand the contents and logic before the final.
comment_teaching: Gibson's teaching is good and quite funny. He will explain the contents and logic clearly. So I enjoy attending his lessons even it is on Zoom.
comment_workload:
The workload is quite low. Every week has one lab work. No attendance required. No other assignment.

You only need to review the contents before the mid-term and final. But if you want a good grade, please finish all the example exercises and understand the logic behind the examples and labs.

So it is a good choice for you to fulfill the common core requirement!

comment_content:
Non-VBA Excel Functions (week 1-3)
Cell References, Operators (^ power, <> not equal to), Logic Functions (and, or, not),String Functions (Concatenation, LEN) IF, VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, LookUp Error

VBA (week 3-12)
Macro, VBA Event, InputBox & MsgBox
Cell References (Cells(Row, Column), ActiveCell - currently selected cell)
IF...Then, Else IF, COUNTIF
Types of Variable (Integer, Long, Single , Double, Boolean)
Loop (While...Wend; Do While...Loop; Do...Loop While, For Loops, (For...To...(Step)...Next),  For Each Loop (For Each...In...Next)
Specifying Input and Output Type, Random Numbers, RANDBETWEEN(a,b)
Local & Global Variables, Excel Objects (Range = cells, Shape), Excel Color (RGB), Doing VBA Events (.Rows.Count, .Value), Brackets for functions with input parameters and output, Private and Public Functions, GUI Control
Object-Oriented Programming (class, attributes, method)
Recursion
comment_teaching:
His teaching is good and easy understand.
Using Zoom, he will also answer our questions typed in chat box.

If you send email to ask, he may also answer your questions.
comment_workload:
There are around 3 to 4 labs that needed to hand in and other do not.

not too many workload
comment_grading:
Maybe due to the online examination, the final is too difficult. There are two papers in final. Both are 90 total in marks.
Mean: 30+ /90
It really shows the difficulties of exam.

The midterm is easy.

The content and VBA is easy in understanding compared with other Comp course so many people get high marks in examinations.

comment_content:
Non-VBA Excel Functions (week 1-3)
Cell References, Operators (^ power, <> not equal to), Logic Functions (and, or, not),String Functions (Concatenation, LEN) IF, VLOOKUP, HLOOKUP, LookUp Error

VBA (week 3-12)
Macro, VBA Event, InputBox & MsgBox
Cell References (Cells(Row, Column), ActiveCell - currently selected cell)
IF...Then, Else IF, COUNTIF
Types of Variable (Integer, Long, Single , Double, Boolean)
Loop (While...Wend; Do While...Loop; Do...Loop While, For Loops, (For...To...(Step)...Next),  For Each Loop (For Each...In...Next)
Specifying Input and Output Type, Random Numbers, RANDBETWEEN(a,b)
Local & Global Variables, Excel Objects (Range = cells, Shape), Excel Color (RGB), Doing VBA Events (.Rows.Count, .Value), Brackets for functions with input parameters and output, Private and Public Functions, GUI Control
Object-Oriented Programming (class, attributes, method)
Recursion

Lab 1: Stock Market Buying and Selling (Excel non-VBA, Excel Functions: AVERAGE, IF, FLOOR)
Lab 2: Moving Around Hong Kong (Excel Non-VBA & VBA, Excel Functions: IF, IFNA error checking,  VLOOKUP, Macro)
Lab 3: 'Escape the Monster' Game (Excel VBA)
(ActiveCell, IF, Cells (Row, Column), variables)
Lab 4: Eliza (Excel VBA)
InStr (Starting Position, From searching area, Target) for returning the position of the target (a number position)
Lab 5: Jigsaw Game (Excel VBA)
Nested loops, random numbers, strings
Lab 6: Music Keyboard (Excel VBA)
Mod - Remainder 7%5 =2, Mod = 2
Mid (from text, starting position, extracted length)
Lab 7: Shape Sculptures
Loops, RGB Color, Interpolations, GUI Control, Mod (Mod 2 = 0) - even numbers

Better to buy a course book to practice more examples on codes
(Excel and Excel VBA Programming for Beginners - by David Rossiter and Gibson Lam) from the bookstore
comment_teaching:
Professor Rossiter has a very interesting, intriguing teaching style, I have attended every lecture of him and I am really enjoy his teaching.
I think he is one of my favorite professor in HKUST over 4 years of study
Lecture videos are provided to every lecture and you can do a revision on it

Questions are asked to award the students who answer correctly and instantly on every lectures as their bonus marks  +0.4% marks per question adding up in your total score (sitting on the front and wearing a special color of cloths will help to raise your chances to answer questions)
comment_workload:
As both Midterm and Final exams are open-book, it indicates the difficulties (that's means even you have all the notes, you may still not know how to do the exam!!!)

But printing all the notes and jotting down all the examples from the lectures are imperative, especially printing all the codes of every lab session (including the last lab!)

Also, do all and print all the Midterm Sample and Final Sample Papers! Make sure you 100% understand why the answers work in a logical way.
I studied a few full days for the final exam to get a better score, to cover my low scores for the midterm.
If I continued to get a -0.83sd in the final, I would be sure I would get a C or C-

Don't look down on the Basics of the course content (the loops, if functions), please have a 100% strong understanding and practice on the basics before moving forward to the advanced

All the teaching materials and examples in lectures, labs and sample papers are relatively easier, YET the EXAMS are totally on a different level!
Again, DON'T UNDERESTIMATE the difficulty of this course!
Programming rookies get a tough time to familiarize the programming language, styles and all the codes.
You will never do a better job as great as those CS majors, people who have studied ICT in DSE. Rookies are coming to put the mean down and favor the high-flyers
comment_grading:
No attendance marks for lectures and labs, but I highly recommend you to go all for bonus marks in lectures, learning the principles and copying all the codes for the lab assignments

Total 7 lab sessions, only 2nd - 6th lab projects count the grades
5 Labs: 5 X 8% = 40%
1st and 7th Lab does not count but the 7th lab about Interpolation is extremely essential for final exam. You must go to the lab session to complete the codes and understand how it works.
The last lab is very difficult for programming rookie like me

Open Book Exams (Both Midterm & Final)
Midterm Exam: 1hr 10mins
Final Exam: 2hrs 10mins
Time is limited and harsh for rookies, please do the exams precisely with a good time management. Don't stick on the questions you don't know.
Read more codes and practice by using the Excel on hands (I got a loop question correct in the final exam, only because I had done a Excel additional practice - sourced from the course website)

If Midterm Result > Final Result
Midterm Exam: 20%
Final Exam: 40%

If Midterm Result < Final Result
Midterm Exam: 0%
Final Exam: 60%

My Case:
Lab: 500/500 = 40 (follow and copy every code given by the TA in lab session to make sure you don't have any mistakes)
Lab is important for rookies to save their grades
Midterm Exam:  56/100 (-0.83sd) (mean 72) (sd: 19.2) = 0%
Final Exam:  64/100 (-0.19sd) (mean 67) (sd: 15.9) = 64 * 60% = 38.4
Bonus mark (Answering questions in the lecture correctly and quickly): 1 question = 0.4
Total = 78.8 = Grade: B-
I only get -0.19sd in the final exam, it already grants me a B-
That's indicate you must get a mean to get a B

When you review on other comments about this course
Total: 77.2 = Grade B- (-0.3sd in final exam)
Total: 76.2 = Grade: C+ (-0.4sd in final exam)
The B-range and C-range are very narrow, the course is extremely competitive, only with total 80% secures you a B
Imagine how many programming experts in this class competing with you
Excel the course with your Logical Intelligence and Carefulness
If you have an unclear logic, you have no any programming background and you are a programming idiot like me, try your best to avoid COMP classes (you go to take other QR Common Core courses, go exchange to transfer credit, or take some distance learning courses)
If not, I will bless for you!

comment_content:
First started with excel formulas and by mid-term student would some basic knowledge of VBA (Mainly creating Loops). After mid-term there would be more content on VBA.
Declaimer : Online course this sem due to pandemic.
comment_teaching: He spoke fast, nonetheless a super lovely lecturer. Still had a great time attending lectures even though its on zoom.
comment_workload: Light workload. Didn't attend any Labs as they were optional. But still shouldn't take a long time to do them on your own.
comment_grading:
Definitely good grades
However keep in mind that the final exams of this course in spring sem have been getting pretty hard. Therefore, I wouldn't say A range comes with ease and would still need to put in some work.

Mid-term : Carried out online with Canvas. In quite a different format compared to the past as we were able to use excel on our own pc during the exam. To ensure fairness, there was only one ' deduce from the codes' kind of question. And I was only able to take advantage of this situation (able to use excel) for one question. Still, mid-term became easier than past years

Final : Also done in Canvas. Two papers, one hour each with 15 mins break in between. This time we weren't able to copy codes from the questions (They added tricks to the codes hence we could only copy a bunch of useless codes). So they put in variety types of questions (just like finals from the past)  and they were difficult.

Labs work (40%) full mark
Midterm (20%) 100/100  Mean: 76.8   SD:~17
Final (40%) 80/100  Mean: 51.2  SD: 14.8   Highest: 96.7
Grade: A+ (Despite 16 marks lower than the highest in the final...)
No bonus mark for answering questions as Zoom lecture this sem.

I had no CS background,  you don't need it to get good grade.

comment_content: To be honest, before i get started writing on this review, i must warn you guys that COMP1022Q is not as good grade as you think. If u have gifted talents in CS,u will definitely do well in this course. But if u are a computer idiot(like me), DO NOT take this course or ur GPA will be spoiled. Actually I got A in other courses but for this so-called "靚grade course", I got an appalling B- grade at the end. Therefore, DO NOT take this course  WITHOUT any thorough consideration. Despite excel being easy, the codes in VBA can go insane in the final exam. So, i just sit there in the exam with arm folded 😢

comment_workload: Light,just finish the lab by following TA instructions,easy to get full mark on that 4 lab assignments

comment_content:
I would say VBA is very interesting at the moment  as I have not finished this course lol. But I would like to make this review because of Cecia <3
For people who like mathematics/ solving logical problems, you will like this course.

As I am not a student with CS-related background, this is my only COMP course in my u life. I would say it is a relatively simple course, easy to understand, train more on your thinking skills rather than memorising things.
comment_teaching:
I am in Gibson class. He is a funny guy, but I think he talked a little bit fast, I could not catch up with his pace (it may be my problem)

Thanks to the online lectures, I can join other lecturers' section. From then, I started attending Cecia's class. I really enjoy her class, she is very patient and so nice. She may include some warm up exercise to help us review the previous lecture content. In general, I really like her teaching. I think her class is very suitable for students who may need more time to digest the concepts.

Also, Peter, our TA, is very nice too. He is delivering the lab content very clearly. and I rmb I emailed him for enquiries, then he replied me at 6am the other day. very responsive and helpful!
comment_workload:
pretty light.
4 labs, midterm and final exam
labs: just pay attention to the TA, then you will get everything done within 1 hour for each lab.
comment_grading:
I have not finished the course, I will update another review at the end of this semester.

for exam, usually exam is more difficult than the practice tests and review questions.

comment_content:
First part was about excel formulas, the later part was about VBA.

All exam questions are short questions and open-book, so what you need is to think logically and give the correct final answers, steps are not important.
comment_teaching: Good professor, you will not feel bored in his lessons.
comment_workload:
Only 4 labs to hand in, and there are step by step guidelines and marking schemes on the website, so it is easy to follow and get high score.

Beside labs, only midterm and final (which was cancelled in this semester).
comment_grading:
Originally, there are two schemes and the better grade from the schemes will be calculated:
labs 40%, midterm 20%, final 40%; or
labs 44%, midterm 0%, final 56%.
In this way you can get a good grade even if you perform not so well in midterm.

However, in this semester, it is changed to:
midterm 33.32%, labs 66.68%

Also, the professor asks questions in the lecture, and randomly picks people to answer the question. You get 0.5% bonus points for each correctly answered question.

For reference, my grades:
labs 100%
midterm 91%
Bonus 2.5 (5 questions answered)
Final grade A+

comment_content: 基本上個課程好小野讀 一開始嗰啲加減乘除同你起中學用計數機冇乜分別 係VBA開始嗰到會多啲啲野 但係其實來來去去都係得幾條式 只要睇得明example基本上就冇大問題
comment_teaching: 佢起堂上面會周不時問人問題如果你怕悶嘅話唔使擔心 但係如果你想毒坐一角就可能麻煩小小 因為佢有時都幾中意抽啲唔係爆四位嘅人來問 大致上佢教學上係好清楚明白解釋得十分詳盡 同埋佢用大量嘅例子來解釋啲內容
comment_workload: 做lab如果上齊堂來做唔會有啲咩大問題 不過如果reg中咗9點嘅lab就可能會辛苦小小
comment_grading:
Mid term 100
Lab 100
Final ~90
Grade A+
雖然佢有兩個scheme 一個計mid term一個唔計 但係佢mid term之前考得容易過final好多 基本上輕輕讀一讀都可以8字頭 所以個人覺得寧願mid term認真啲去考 final輕鬆小小會更加開心

comment_content:
Basic formulae of Excel
Then head to VBA part
Not much hard concept.
comment_teaching: He creates a very good atmosphere in every lesson by making jokes. He teaches very well as he always makes good explanation and demonstrations.
comment_workload: Very little workload as lab work is very straight forward, only need to answer easy questions every lesson to get a bonus point.
comment_grading:
Very good grading as all exams are open book exam.
Very easy to get A range if you try to do the past paper he gave and print out all the powerpoint and codes of the lab work.

comment_content: VBA programming language specifically in Excel. An interesting although not entirely pragmatic course that teaches fundamental programming skills in entertaining ways. Content can pretty much be self-taught as it is not overtly difficult and his power points are concise and useful.
comment_teaching: By far the most entertaining teacher I've had at UST so far. He has a quirky yet amiable sense of humor that makes the lectures seem to go by so fast. He frequently asks students questions pertaining to what he is teaching which can earn you an extra 0.5% to add on to your final grade if you get them right, or if he thinks you've tried hard enough to answer. His English proficiency is in a whole nother category of its own in comparison to most UST professors.
comment_workload: Very managble. On average there is about one Lab assignment which can take 30mins to 2hrs depending on the specific one. Most require overlapping skills that are taught to you throughout the course so by the end of it you'll be easily able to complete the, without going to tutorials (they're optional).
comment_grading: You'll attain a good grade assuming you manage to score high on the relatively easy exams. Only issue would be that the means are so high that even small errors can cost you a while grade, but this in it of itself shouldn't be an issue as the questions asked in the Midterms and Finals are not that hard. Also the Lab assignments are provided to us with a mark scheme so its easy to check and predict your scores for them.

comment_content: Content is quite useful and helps you to get a bit of understanding of what programming languages are like.
comment_teaching: He has a clear teaching style. Easy to understand.
comment_workload: Not much workload. But better attend labs so that you don't need to do the lab assignments by yourself.
comment_grading: Not hard to get A. But make sure to get perfect scores on all lab assignments and review all ppts and that you can apply the knowledge in various situations. It is important if you can actually apply the theories into real life, unexpected situation, not the theories itself!

comment_content:
Before mid-term: basic excel (easy), and basic VBA
After mid-term: More difficult VBA
comment_teaching: Rossiter 教得幾好，個人好funny，上堂氣氛好好，會上堂叫人答問題，唔識答唔緊要，答左會有bouns mark

comment_content:
Easy content. Include basic VBA instruction and some excel function.
Pretty simple for those who have programming background or those who studied ICT in secondary school. Even if you have no previous knowledge , it is easy to catch up.
comment_teaching: Good.David tries a lot to interact with the students and ask some questions.
comment_workload: Few easy assignments. Each take less than an hour to be done. Just follow the guidelines.
comment_grading:
Should be pretty good.
But due to university policy this semester, the final is canceled. Only assignments and mid term is counted. The cut off of the grades thus are high.
There are bonus mark in answering questions, these can be really helpful in achieving higher sub-grade.

comment_content: a lot of basic stuff but really useful at the same time. actual programming starts in the second month.
comment_teaching: David is a wonderful professor, teaches in a funny way. there is a course website where you can find the materials for the course and teacher assistants will upload videos to assist in your labs.
comment_workload: only 5 compulsory labs I think, for the first 3 labs, the teacher assistants will go through them with you 100%
comment_grading: if you attend most of the classes, a b grade is guaranteed. Grades are based on midterms, finals, labs and additional questions asked by the professor during lectures
